question_text: What happens to light when it passes from air into a glass block?

options.label: A
options.text: It bends away from the normal

options.label: C
options.text: It travels in a straight line without bending

explanation: When light enters a denser medium like glass from a less dense medium like air, it slows down and bends towards the normal (an imaginary line perpendicular to the surface). This effect is called refraction.
